'''Magus''' were a peaceful [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] found in the [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Reopi]]. They were [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] for their hide, which they used along with noxious secretions to defend themselves from predators. They hunted [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] and [[Insect/Legends|insects]] by burrowing.

==Biology and appearance==
Magus were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] browsers found in the equatorial [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Reopi]],<ref name="CotG" /> a world in a [[Reopi system/Legends|star system]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Peaceful and solitary by nature, they survived on a [[Diet/Legends|diet]] of [[Insect/Legends|insects]] and small [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]], which they hunted by burrowing rapidly through shifting sands using their two rubbery, webbed hind [[Limb/Legends|feet]]. When fully extended, members of the species measured fifty-eight [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] in length, but they sometimes stood with a hunched posture on their hind limbs, not using their two smaller front limbs for support.<ref name="CotG" />

Besides burrowing, magus possessed several other methods of avoiding or deterring predators like the [[Reopi sand crusher]], including a tough and durable hide, claws on all four limbs, and a gland capable of releasing noxious [[Oil/Legends|oil]] secretions. These secretions were used to maintain suppleness in the outer layers of the creature's hide but also drove off most predators within three or four [[Meter/Legends|meters]] of the magus.<ref name="CotG" />

==History==
Magus hide was valued as a material on many less technologically advanced worlds, and the creatures were legally [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] for the resource, although under quota regulation and only by private businesses based on Reopi. As one of the last animals hunted for commercial value in the Inner Rim, magus were most vulnerable to hunters while drinking at oases. Their natural defenses and the strict regulation of their hunting protected the magus population from diminishing.<ref name="CotG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Magus was first featured in ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', a sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' released in [[1994]]. The concept and illustration of a magus was provided by [[Mike Vilardi]], while it was designed by [[Rick D. Stuart]].<ref name="CotG" />
